mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rate levels increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raise debt-service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its historical median, or the Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS and 20-day returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market deterioration with unemployment above 5.5% reflects falling aggregate income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the triggered strategy Sharpe ratio is not significantly below zero (p > 0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with rising unemployment reflects monetary policy tightening into economic weakness, which reprices recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A long-short portfolio sorted on the interaction of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE changes produces negative annualized alpha over any 3-year window, or the interaction term coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) in a 20-day return regression.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through earnings surprise channels in consumer-led expansions.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth positively predicts 5-day returns in less than 55% of consumer-led expansion months, or the rank correlation between RSAFS changes and 5-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in industrial production reflect stronger manufacturing earnings and capacity utilization, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO changes show a negative rank correlation with 5-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of months with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 0.5% reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances (GDPC1 QoQ > 0.5%) produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 45% of the time, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect construction sector strength and positive wealth effects, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST changes exhibit a negative rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or rising HOUST coincides with positive 20-day returns less than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with a low nominal federal funds rate forces future monetary tightening to close the negative real rate gap, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%
failure_condition: The joint signal of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS does not generate negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) in rolling regressions.
